content.full_text:
. 4 SK. California

Memorandum

To : Mr. Ted Prim
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Date: August 3, 1976

From : Secretary of State

Subject: FPPC Regulation 18901 -
Publicly Financed Mass Mailings

Upon reading the FPPC May 25 memo and Regulation 18901, there
are several items concerning the Secretary of State's Office
which require consideration and/or interpretation.

1. Section 3301 of the Corporations Code (Section
1502, effective January 1, 1977) requires every
domestic corporation within 90 days after filing
articles of incorporation and annually there-
after, to file with the Secretary of State, a
statement setting forth the number of corporate
directors, the name and business address of each
director, and the names and addresses of corpo-
rate officers, etc.

The statute says the report must be on a form
prescribed by the Secretary of State. It does
not say the Secretary of State must provide the
form. However, from a practical viewpoint, the
Secretary of State must and does provide the
form on which to report. To do otherwise would
result in dramatic noncompliance. The statutes
allow the Secretary of State to administratively
charge a modest fee to fully recover the cost
of the entire program element.

Given there are in excess of a quarter million
corporations, the mailing of the forms appears
to constitute mass mailing as defined by Section
82041.5 of the Government Code. The Secretary
of State's name does not appear on the form;
however, it does appear on the envelope, which is
standard practice for all items mailed from this
office.

[153]

Mr. Ted Prim                    - 2 -                    August 3, 1976


     2.  A similar concern exists concerning forms used by
         candidates and committees who are required to
         file reports with this office pursuant to provi-
         sions of Prop. 9.

         The Act does not require our office to mail forms
         to the filers, but we do so in the best interest
         of the program.  Here again, the Secretary of
         State's name appears on the mailing envelope.

     3.  Section 18901(b)(2) & (3) refers to letterhead and
         stationery.  Does the word "stationery" include
         envelopes?  If it doesn't, it in my judgment should.

The above examples are not intended to be all inclusive, but
they do represent our concern about the mass mailing regulation.

May we please hear from you concerning this matter?

                                    [illegible]
                                    R. J. Nannini
                                    Assistant Secretary of State

RJN:jp

It does not say the Secretary of State must provide the form. However, from a practical viewpoint, the Secretary of State must and does provide the form on which to report. To do otherwise would result in dramatic noncompliance. The statutes allow the Secretary of State to administratively charge a modest fee to fully recover the cost of the entire program element. Given there are in excess of a quarter million corporations, the mailing of the forms appears to constitute mass mailing as defined by Section 82041.5 of the Government Code. The Secretary of State's name does not appear on the form; however, it does appear on the envelope, which is standard practice for all items mailed from this office. The image you provided is extremely faint and mostly illegible due to excessive brightness, likely caused by overexposure or high contrast. There are very few visible characters or text that can be reliably transcribed. If you have another scan with better quality or another page, please provide it for better results. 2. A similar concern exists concerning forms used by candidates and committees who are required to file reports with this office pursuant to provisions of Prop. 9. The Act does not require our office to mail forms to the filers, but we do so in the best interest of the program. Here again, the Secretary of State's name appears on the mailing envelope. 3. Nannini Assistant Secretary of State RJN:jp
